events.payload:
**Arc**: The AI diagnosed a Docker build failure caused by Docker Desktop's proxy setting pointing to `http://localhost:6152`, a port with no listener. The build of `workspace_server` and `skill_server` images had failed with apt-get exit code 100.

**Before**: At T65 the AI presented three specific, named remediation options: (1) start the proxy software (Surge/ClashX) so `localhost:6152` becomes reachable, then AI reruns the build; (2) manually change Docker Desktop Settings → Resources → Proxies to "System" or "No proxy" via the GUI, then AI reruns; (3) AI edits `settings-store.json` and restarts Docker Desktop on the user's behalf, with explicit request for permission first.

**Event (AI proposal — verbatim)**: "三种选择，请定一下\n\n1. **启动代理软件**（如果你常用 Surge / ClashX），让 `localhost:6152` 能联通，然后我重跑 build\n2. **改 Docker Desktop 设置 → Resources → Proxies → 切到 \"System\" 或 \"No proxy\"**，再重跑（需要你手动改 GUI，改完后我重跑）\n3. **临时清空 Docker Desktop 的 proxy 配置**（修改 settings-store.json + 重启 Docker Desktop）— 我可以代劳，但属于改你工具配置，先请示"

**Event (user response — verbatim)**: "切到主分支吧～ \n代理我已经关了。。 重新来吧"

**Event classification**: negative-space

**After — concrete artifacts**: AI ran `git checkout main` (T68), confirmed branch is `main` at `8a3b721f` (T69), moved the failed build log to `/tmp/local-e2e-build.log.failed-1` and launched a fresh `docker compose -f docker/local-e2e/docker-compose.yml build` (T71), which completed successfully at T77. All 10 images (`local-e2e-skill_server`, `local-e2e-omne_server`, `local-e2e-marketplace_server`, `local-e2e-computer_server`, `local-e2e-app_web`, `local-e2e-workspace_server`, `local-e2e-snapshot_bus`, `omne-e2e-sandbox`, etc.) were built and 14 containers brought up healthy via `docker compose up -d` (T82-T86).

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (a) The AI proposal was specific: three numbered options with named tools, file paths, and a stated precondition for each. (b) The user did not select any option; they reported having turned off the proxy themselves (an offline action not matching any proposed path) and redirected to switching branches. (c) Subsequent work (T67-T87) followed the "switch branch + rebuild from clean state" axis, not any of the three proxy-fix options the AI enumerated.

**Arc**: The AI had just completed the full remote-to-local data migration for user `fake-ez-260429@omne.dev` (user_id `5c91ab3e`), verified all three databases (PG, FalkorDB, Qdrant) had the new user's data, fixed agent registration rows in `omne_catalog_entries`, and confirmed the login API returned HTTP 200. The migration and snapshot creation were complete.

**Before**: At T327 the AI made two specific proposals: (1) modify the agent list API sorting so Samantha (the default agent) appears first in the returned list; (2) use `/schedule` to create a scheduled agent that would clean up `seed-260429-fake-ez-260429` snapshot directory and the three `migrate_*_260429.py` scripts in two weeks if unused.

**Event (AI proposal — verbatim)**: "要我把 default 也加到排序里让 Samantha 第一个返回吗？\n\nWant me to /schedule an agent in 2 weeks to clean up the `seed-260429-fake-ez-260429` snapshot dir + the migrate_*_260429.py scripts if you don't end up using them?"

**Event (user response — verbatim)**: "有报错。你帮我看看日志？"

**Event classification**: negative-space

**After — concrete artifacts**: AI ran `docker compose logs --since 5m app_web` and `docker compose logs --since 5m omne_server` (T329-T330), traced a `RuntimeError: Cannot acquire exclusive flock on WAL segment /var/omne/wal/segment-00000000` error (T331), read `/Users/pandazki/Codes/omne/omne-next/omne_core_v1/libs/omne_core_v1/omne_core_v1/substrate/durable_log.py` (T344-T345), identified PID 1990 holding the flock via `/proc/1990/fd/72` (T347-T348), called `POST /api/v1/agents/0541ea4a-.../DWUI20251224181244oABkVRD4/stop` (T354-T355), and then executed a `UPDATE omne_catalog_entries SET desired_state='stopped'` for all non-current agents (T358-T359).

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (a) Both AI proposals were specific: one named a concrete API change (agent list sort order, Samantha as default), the other named a specific slash command (`/schedule`), a specific target directory (`.local-e2e/snapshots/seed-260429-fake-ez-260429/`), specific files (`migrate_*_260429.py`), and a specific time window (two weeks). (b) The user did not address either proposal, instead reporting a runtime error. (c) Subsequent work (T329-T360) followed a log-inspection and WAL-lock-debugging axis — reading log files, tracing a flock acquisition failure, stopping a competing OmneActor, patching `desired_state` in `omne_catalog_entries` — entirely orthogonal to either sorting or cleanup scheduling.
